label: Icon Button
purpose: Circular icon-only action button. Wraps any Lucide icon (or inline SVG) in a 32×32px circle with a transparent background. Hover and active states reveal --bg-accent-dim (dim yellow). label prop is always required and becomes aria-label. Renders as <button> only — never <a>.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible name for the button. Becomes aria-label on the root <button> element. Required — omitting it fails WCAG SC 4.1.2. Must describe the action, not the icon (e.g. 'Copy email address', not 'Copy icon').

props.name: class
props.description: Appended to 'icon-btn' via class:list. For one-off overrides only. Do not use to change background, width, height, or border-radius — those are controlled by .icon-btn in homepage.css.

props.name: ...rest
props.description: Spread onto the <button> element. Use for onClick, type, disabled, data-* attributes.

variantNote: No named variants. There is one visual form. Hover and active states are CSS-driven and automatic.

states.name: hover
states.description: --bg-accent-dim (#E8F23D at reduced opacity) background, icon color unchanged

states.name: active
states.description: Same as hover (background visible, no additional transform)

dimensions.note: Dimensions are hard-coded in .icon-btn. Do not override width, height, or border-radius inline.

renderRule: Always renders as <button>. Never <a>. For icon-based navigation links, use Link with an icon in the slot.
accessibility: label is never optional — aria-label is the only accessible name; there is no visible text label
label must describe the action, not the icon shape ('Copy email address', not 'Copy')
Icon must be aria-hidden — in .astro files, Lucide icons from @lucide/astro are aria-hidden by default; in .jsx files using lucide-react, add aria-hidden={true} to the icon
32×32px minimum touch target is enforced by .icon-btn — do not shrink it
Focus ring (2px --color-focus outline) must not be suppressed — do not add outline:none
whenToUse: Icon-only actions where the action context makes the intent clear (copy, share, dismiss, bookmark, like, more options)
Toolbar-style rows of multiple actions where visible text labels would be too dense
Utility actions adjacent to content (copy email, share article)
whenNotToUse: When a visible text label is available — use Button instead; visible labels are always more accessible than icon-only
As a navigation link — use Link; IconButton always renders <button>, never <a>
At a size other than 32×32px — dimensions are hard-coded; do not resize with inline styles
With text content in the slot — the slot is for an icon only; text alongside an icon breaks the circular shape

iconGuidance.inAstroFiles: Import from @lucide/astro. Use size={20} (no braces needed in Astro: size=20).
iconGuidance.inJSXFiles: Import from lucide-react. Use size={20} with JSX braces. Add aria-hidden={true} to the icon element.
iconGuidance.inlineSVG: Acceptable. Set width='20' height='20'. Add aria-hidden='true' to the SVG.

knownRawCSSMimics.migrationNote: Footer.jsx is a React island — cannot import IconButton.astro. The raw pattern in Footer.jsx is the documented correct approach for React contexts: <button type='button' className='icon-btn' aria-label='...'><Icon size={20} aria-hidden={true} /></button>.

usageConstraints.reason: IconButton.astro cannot be imported into .jsx React islands. In React files, use a raw <button className='icon-btn' aria-label='...'> with a lucide-react icon at size={20} and aria-hidden={true}. This is the documented fallback pattern.
